content.thinking:
The finding is about a fabrication in s3's note. The note says `UPDATED_USER_ID（同）` but the source says `REGISTER_USER_ID` for 更新ユーザID. I need to fix this in the note at the end of s3.

Current note: `INSERT_USER_ID（事前準備データのUSER_ID）、UPDATED_USER_ID（同）、INSERT_DATE・UPDATE_DATE（コンポーネント設定ファイルに記載した固定システム日付）も更新対象として検証する。`

The source says: `更新ユーザID(REGISTER_USER_ID)と更新日付(UPDATE_DATE)も更新対象としている。`


There's a mismatch between what the SQL column is named and what the source documentation calls it—the source explicitly refers to the column as `REGISTER_USER_ID` for 更新ユーザID, even though the actual SQL uses `UPDATED_USER_ID`.

The knowledge file incorrectly adopted the SQL column name `UPDATED_USER_ID（同）` instead of following the source note's terminology, so I need to correct it back to `REGISTER_USER_ID（同）` to align with the original documentation.

content.text: The fix is in s3's note: change `UPDATED_USER_ID（同）` to `REGISTER_USER_ID（同）` to match the source note which explicitly names the column `REGISTER_USER_ID`.

index.title: 精査処理呼び出し実装

content.text: Fixed: In s3's note, changed `UPDATED_USER_ID（同）` → `REGISTER_USER_ID（同）` to match the source note which explicitly states `更新ユーザID(REGISTER_USER_ID)`.
